full_text:
A black kite cools off in the shade near water, showing signs of dehydration. Common indicators of heat stress in birds include panting, drooping wings, lethargy, rapid breathing and in severe cases, collapse or seizures
At Bai Sakarbai Dinshaw Petit Hospital for Animals, over 70 birds and nearly 20 dogs and cats have been treated for dehydration in early March, including an injured barn owl and a dehydrated crow seen resting during recovery
Petit Hospital staff and volunteers carefully rescued a distressed Brahminy kite, highlighting the importance of timely intervention during heatwaves
Household pets, including dogs and cats, are also affected by heat stress, making up 20 percent of dehydration cases at the hospital
Simple care steps like moving animals to shade, sprinkling water, letting them rest, and contacting rescue services can save lives during heatwaves
Citizens like Harshita Charatker and her family rescued a barn owl found on the road, demonstrating the importance of community action in animal welfare.

title: Heatwave Warning: Mumbai Hospitals Report Surge In Heatstroke Cases As Doctors Highlight Serious Risks To - freepressjournal.in
full_text:
Heatwave Warning: Mumbai Hospitals Report Surge In Heatstroke Cases As Doctors Highlight Serious Risks To Brain Health
Hospitals in Mira-Bhayandar are seeing a surge in heatstroke, dehydration and neurological symptoms amid rising temperatures. Doctors warn that extreme heat can impair brain function, causing dizziness, confusion and even stroke-like conditions. Experts urge hydration, avoiding peak heat hours and early symptom recognition to prevent serious complications.
Mira-Bhayandar: As temperatures continue to climb across the city, hospitals are witnessing a sharp rise in cases of heatstroke, dehydration, and heat-related neurological complications. Medical experts warn that the ongoing heatwave is not only straining physical health but also posing serious risks to brain function.
Alarming Symptoms
According to healthcare professionals at Wockhardt Hospitals, Mira Road, an increasing number of patients are reporting symptoms such as dizziness, confusion, persistent headaches, fatigue, and fainting spells. These early signs of heat exhaustion, if ignored, can rapidly progress to life-threatening heatstroke.
Dr. Pavan Pai, Consultant Interventional Neurologist and Stroke Specialist, highlighted the growing concern. “Extreme heat can significantly impact brain health. Dehydration and electrolyte imbalances interfere with normal brain function, leading to poor concentration, mood disturbances, and, in severe cases, stroke-like symptoms,” he said.
Temperature Sensitivity
He further explained that the brain is highly sensitive to temperature fluctuations. When the body overheats, it can disrupt blood flow to the brain and impair neural activity, making timely hydration and cooling measures essential.
Doctors have identified several high-risk groups, including children, senior citizens, and individuals with pre-existing neurological or cardiovascular conditions. Outdoor workers such as construction labourers, traffic police personnel, and delivery workers are particularly vulnerable due to prolonged exposure to direct sunlight and high humidity levels.
“Even mild dehydration can affect memory, alertness, and decision-making abilities,” Dr. Pai added. “In extreme cases, heatstroke may lead to brain swelling, seizures, or permanent neurological damage.”
Experts recommend simple yet effective preventive measures, including staying well-hydrated, consuming electrolyte-rich fluids, avoiding outdoor activity during peak afternoon hours, and ensuring adequate rest.
With Mumbai grappling with rising temperatures and high humidity, health professionals stress that protecting brain health is just as crucial as safeguarding the body. Awareness, early recognition of symptoms, and prompt action remain key to preventing serious complications during the summer months.
